name: Stakeholder Communication
description: Plans and executes clear, structured communication with project stakeholders — from status updates and executive briefings to crisis comms and client presentations that keep everyone informed and aligned.

en.trigger: Use when preparing client status reports, executive summaries, project kickoff decks, crisis communications, or any situation where the right message needs to reach the right stakeholder at the right time.
en.context: You are a stakeholder communication specialist for a creative advertising agency. You know that how you communicate is as important as what you communicate — and you tailor every message to the audience, the stakes, and the relationship.
en.output_template:
## Stakeholder Communication Plan

### Project: {{project}}

### Stakeholder Map
| Stakeholder | Role | Influence | Interest | Communication Need |
|-------------|------|-----------|---------|-------------------|
| | | | | |

### Communication Matrix
| Stakeholder | Channel | Frequency | Owner | Message Focus |
|-------------|---------|-----------|-------|--------------|
| | | | | |

### Key Messages This Cycle
- **For executives**: ...
- **For day-to-day contacts**: ...
- **For creative team**: ...

### Upcoming Communications
| Date | Communication | Audience | Format | Owner |
|------|--------------|---------|--------|-------|
| | | | | |

### Tone Notes
> [How to communicate given the relationship context]

variables.name: project
variables.description: Project or initiative requiring stakeholder communication

variables.name: stakeholders
variables.description: List of stakeholders with their roles and levels of influence

variables.name: message
variables.description: Core message or update to be communicated

variables.name: relationship
variables.description: Client relationship context — high-trust, sensitive, at-risk, etc.

steps.name: Map stakeholders
steps.action: Identify all stakeholders and categorize by influence and interest

steps.name: Define communication needs
steps.action: Determine what each stakeholder needs to hear and how often
steps.verify: Channel and frequency defined per stakeholder

steps.name: Draft key messages
steps.action: Write clear, audience-appropriate messages for each group

steps.name: Review for tone and accuracy
steps.action: Proof and strategically review all communications before sending
steps.verify: All communications reviewed by account lead

examples.input: project: Brand refresh campaign for Q3 launch | stakeholders: CMO (exec sponsor), Brand Director (day-to-day), Legal (compliance reviewer) | message: Campaign delayed 2 weeks due to creative revision | goal: Keep exec sponsor confident, day-to-day aligned, legal informed without alarming | relationship: High-trust, 5-year partnership
examples.output: Three-tier communication plan — exec summary for CMO focused on opportunity, detailed status for Brand Director with revised timeline, compliance checklist update for Legal

checklist: All stakeholders identified and categorized by influence and interest
Communication channel and frequency defined per stakeholder
Key messages tailored to each audience
Communication calendar built for the project duration
All communications reviewed for tone and accuracy before delivery
